notes: SFMTA Director of Transportation -> SFMTA Board of Directors. Staff develops and presents fare/budget proposals for Board action.
Mayor of San Francisco -> SFMTA Board of Directors. Mayoral appointment power makes the Mayor an indirect governance actor.
Mayor of San Francisco -> SFMTA Director of Transportation. Budget expectations and public messaging flow from City Hall.
Board of Supervisors -> Mayor of San Francisco. Budget negotiations and override politics shape final appropriations.
Board of Supervisors Budget and Appropriations Committee -> San Francisco Controller's Office. Fiscal analysis strongly shapes committee debate.
San Francisco Controller's Office -> Mayor of San Francisco. Controller-led deficit framing affects what is seen as credible.
San Francisco Controller's Office -> Board of Supervisors. Working-group and fiscal materials help set the field of acceptable options.
San Francisco County Transportation Authority -> SFMTA Director of Transportation. SFCTA is a key venue for revenue strategy and Muni fiscal updates.
Metropolitan Transportation Commission -> SFMTA. MTC administers regional transit funding and the emergency bridge-loan structure.
Governor/CalSTA -> Metropolitan Transportation Commission. State-authorized loan is routed through MTC.
Transit riders and rider coalitions -> Board of Supervisors. Public testimony and district pressure can shape supervisor positions.
Transit riders and rider coalitions -> SFMTA Board of Directors. Rider testimony is influential during fare and service hearings.
Youth advocates and school/community organizations -> Transit riders and rider coalitions. Youth frame broadens equity coalition.
Senior and disability advocates -> Transit riders and rider coalitions. Access arguments become stronger when grounded in existing programs.
Transit labor unions -> Mayor of San Francisco. Labor can support or complicate a transit package depending on staffing implications.
Transit labor unions -> Board of Supervisors. Labor endorsements and opposition matter in city budget politics.
Downtown/business groups -> Local parcel tax proposal. Commercial-property costs are a likely tension point.
SPUR and policy think-tank voices -> Board of Supervisors. Elite-policy endorsements can make transit revenue proposals sound more mainstream.
Local media and editorial boards -> Mayor of San Francisco. Media framing affects political risk calculations.
Local media and editorial boards -> Board of Supervisors. Editorial narratives can amplify fiscal skepticism or access framing.
